steps.title: Apply the fractional exponent rule
steps.content: A power of $\tfrac{1}{2}$ is the same as a square root, and the exponent acts on the entire base $57y$, not just one factor:$$\left(57y\right)^{1/2}=\sqrt{57y}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $57\sqrt{y}$ only takes the root of $y$ and leaves the 57 outside the radical — not equivalent.
  • B) $\sqrt{57}\cdot y$ only takes the root of 57 and leaves $y$ outside — not equivalent.
  • C) $\sqrt{57y}$ is exactly the rewrite of $(57y)^{1/2}$.
  • D) $\sqrt{(57y)^2}=57y$ for $y>0$, which is the original base, not its square root.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
